channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 inhibitors may improve left ventricular diastolic function in hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
item.description: 1. Sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 inhibitors (SGLT-2i) were associated with improved left ventricular diastolic function and New York Heart Associatio...

item.title: State public-option health plans expand but can't fill gaps left by federal changes
item.description: Nevada recently became the third state to offer one of the plans on the ACA marketplaces. They're intended to be a cheaper insurance option but so far...

item.title: 8 creative ways to build your village, according to our listeners
item.description: NPR listeners share how they've made relationships with their neighbors and community. Many of them, through parties, potlucks and coffees, say they'v...

item.title: FDA clears Medtronic stealth AXiS AI-Robotic spine system
item.description: 1. The Stealth AXiS™ system has received regulatory clearance as the first platform to natively unify AI-driven planning and robotic execution for spi...

item.title: This form of mental exercise may cut dementia risk for decades
item.description: A study finds that people who did one specific form of brain training in the 1990s were less likely to be diagnosed with dementia over the next 20 yea...

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg to face jury in landmark social media addiction trial
item.description: The case is seen as a test of social media's legal responsibility for platform design features that plaintiffs' lawyers say exacerbated mental health ...

item.title: Vascularized liver tissueoid-on-a-chip models regeneration and transplant rejection
item.description: Dr. Vadim Jucaud's lab at the Terasaki Institute has developed a vascularized liver tissueoid-on-a-chip (LToC) platform that recapitulates key structu...

item.title: Study reveals low rates of routine screening for anxiety, intimate partner violence in Oregon
item.description: Despite national guidelines recommending routine screening for anxiety and intimate partner violence in women and adolescent girls, a new study from O...

item.title: NHS Rations Bone Cement as Supply Gap Looms
item.description: A global bone cement supply fault could delay hip and knee replacements, with NHS stocks lasting just weeks. Press Association

item.title: Managing ‘Heavy Legs’ in Chronic Venous Insufficiency
item.description: Conservative management remains the foundation of chronic venous insufficiency care and reduces the risk for ulceration when initiated early. Medscape...

item.title: Ambiguous billing rules threaten every doctor in practice [PODCAST]

item.title: Trauma reactivation: Why news headlines trigger past abuse
item.description: She did not begin with the abuse; she began with sleep. “I do not know why the news is bothering me so much,” she said. “I cannot turn it off. I feel ...

item.title: Missing ANGPTL4 gene in mice reprograms immunity, shielding against gut inflammation
item.description: A novel study using a mouse model has found that the absence of the angiopoietin-like 4 (ANGPTL4) protein during development triggers a long-lasting r...

item.title: Pharmacy Closures Threaten Community Care Shift
item.description: Two thirds of pharmacies in England are operating at a loss, raising fears of service cuts and further closures, the NPA has warned. Medscape News UK

item.title: Prenatal lead exposure related to worse cognitive function in adults
item.description: Exposure to lead before birth is associated with lower performance 60 years later on tests of thinking and memory skills in female participants, accor...

item.title: FDA Approves Monthly Dosing of Rybrevant Faspro for NSCLC
item.description: The agent, first approved in December for biweekly subcutaneous injection for EGFR-mutated NSCLC, showed similar efficacy and safety when administered...

item.title: Bimekizumab Eases Plaque Psoriasis Symptoms Up to 3 Years
item.description: Patients with moderate‑to‑severe plaque psoriasis achieve greater relief from itching, skin pain, and scaling with bimekizumab vs secukinumab up to 3 ...

item.title: Neighborhood factors related to financial stress are linked to worse breast cancer outcomes
item.description: Research published in JAMA Network Open connects multiple residential factors generally associated with financial strain, such as high housing costs a...

item.title: A safe space for girls who've faced sexual violence
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo is seeing a significant increase in acts of sexual violence against girls and young women. A support center offers a ...

item.title: One in Six Medicare Beneficiaries Use Telehealth for Essential Care

item.title: Exposure to air pollution is associated with increased risk of developing limitations in physical function
item.description: 1. Greater long-term exposure to particulate matter (PM) and nitrogen dioxide (NO2) was associated with increased risk of progressing from no disabili...

item.title: Value-based care data gap: Why metrics fail to reach the bedside
item.description: Value-based care represents one of health care’s most important transformations, driving hospitals and health systems to focus on outcomes that truly ...

item.title: Deprescribing in health care: Why less medication can be more
item.description: Every day, millions of Americans take medications to manage chronic conditions or prevent disease. Prescription drugs are vital tools in modern medici...

item.title: What the folinic acid retraction means for autism treatment
item.description: On January 29, 2026, the European Journal of Pediatrics formally retracted what had been the largest randomized clinical trial examining folinic acid ...
